summary: Current-HEAD strict-bound verification and focused replay support safe waiver propagation; stale, malformed, and critical waiver paths fail closed.
inspection_summary: Inspected Story/Architecture, authorization and merge wiring, acceptance/integration tests, and current pr-prepare verification state at frozen HEAD 00014677d51664005b8cd0be5aa0a1f7aab078d3; reran focused acceptance tests.
inspection_evidence: Focused contract/E2E replay passed 6/6; production CLI replay passed 3/3. Current pr-prepare verification lists unit, integration, e2e, typecheck, and build as pass with strict_head binding to 00014677d51664005b8cd0be5aa0a1f7aab078d3. merge-manager accepts pr-create authority only through isCurrentPrLifecycleArtifact, then records gate_authorization in pr-merge; stale-waiver CLI fixture asserts blocked gate_not_ready even with explicit PR selector.
judgment_delta: Initial concern: waiver propagation could bypass current-HEAD or external merge safeguards. Final conclusion: current pr-create freshness is enforced before policy evaluation, malformed/missing-policy/unknown-or-critical gate lists reject, and GitHub checks/review/base/remote-head preconditions remain independent AND conditions.
